purpose: Understand the problem, target users, market context, constraints, and value hypothesis before solution design.
outputs: Approved requirement and story decomposition for the Change Observatory
validation: feature proposal links to user value
The requirement captures installability, project lineage, explainability boundaries, security, accessibility, packaging, and verification.

contextualization.summary: Define an install-integrated, local-first Change Observatory for Agentic SDLC that reconstructs what was requested, decided, changed, verified, and released across stories, phase steps, and iterations from canonical project evidence.

# Agentic SDLC Codex Plugin Agentic SDLC 0.6.0 gives Codex a guided way to understand an existing software project and deliver a verified technical or functional assessment. The normal experience is intentionally simple Codex explains what it inferred proposes the work in plain language creates the requested real file verifies it and returns an auditable result. Project state stays in the target repository under `.sdlc/`. The plugin installation contains only reusable skills templates schemas and the cross-platform Node.js CLI. ## Documentation Map - Documentation home (docs/README.md) — choose the right guide without knowing the internal record names. - How It Works (docs/how-it-works.md) — the complete two-checkpoint journey state transitions authorization verification and recovery. - Autonomy Limits and Metering (docs/limits-and-metering.md) — concrete time step token cost reserve warning and stop-policy examples. - Assessment Interactions (docs/agent-interactions.md) — the precise contract used for every user question. - Portable Installation (docs/portable-install.md) — installation update diagnosis and recovery on supported platforms. ## Quick Start Install from the `aantenor

# Architecture Agentic SDLC separates the reusable method from project-specific knowledge. ```text Codex plugin -> skill instructions -> templates -> schemas -> cross-platform CLI Target project -> .sdlc/ -> baseline -> assessments -> budgets -> contracts -> authorizations -> authorization-uses -> receipts -> capability-discovery -> output-contracts -> work-items -> work-breakdown -> dependencies -> stories -> orchestration -> locks -> handoffs -> decisions -> traces -> tests -> releases -> manifests -> archive -> cache -> indexes ``` ```mermaid flowchart TB subgraph Plugin Reusable plugin Skill Skill instructions Templates Templates Schemas Schemas CLI Cross-platform CLI end subgraph Project Target project KB .sdlc source of truth Baseline Project baseline Assessments Assessment proposals and workflows Receipts Approval use generation verification and budget receipts Capabilities Capability discovery OutputRegistry Output contracts registry Breakdown Work breakdown agreements Dependencies Dependency graph Traces Append-only traces Manifests KB manifests Archive Archive plans Cache Local cache Indexes Search indexes end Skill --> CLI Templates --> CLI Schemas --> CLI CLI --> KB CLI

questions.question: How is it delivered?
questions.answer: Inside the installable plugin with a stable CLI launcher and zero manual build.

questions.question: What evidence is authoritative?
questions.answer: Canonical .sdlc records and explicitly linked Git evidence; generated explanations must cite their source records.

questions.question: May it expose private chain-of-thought?
questions.answer: No. It may show recorded rationale, alternatives, inputs, outputs, and evidence only.

constraints: No mandatory cloud service or external data transfer.
Read-only loopback serving by default with safe path containment.
Preserve the dependency-free Node.js runtime unless an approved change proves necessary.
assumptions: The current project has an initialized .sdlc knowledge base; partial and legacy records must degrade gracefully.

approvals.summary: Approved under Antonio's delegated Change Observatory scope; the brief is complete, local-only, install-integrated, evidence-bound, and excludes private chain-of-thought.

scope.principle: An automation approval is valid only under an explicit delegated approval level or configured automation policy recorded in the summary or evidence.
scope.approval_level: Change Observatory discovery only: canonicalize the displayed project-specific discovery brief and confirm requirement intake; excludes installs, external services, deployment, secrets, destructive actions, and unrelated work.
